sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a history of low back pain that started approximately 2 hours after raking leaves in the yard, with symptoms of pins and needles in the right foot.
The patient reports that the pain improved with a hot shower and sitting.
The patient has a history of similar back pain 3 years ago that resolved after a day.
The patient's partner reports that the patient's pain is exacerbated by standing up.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of back pain that resolved after a day, 3 years ago.
Review of systems: The patient reports tingling sensation in the right leg.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's dorsiflexion was normal, and pulses were equal in all extremities.
The patient reported pain with flexion, palpation around the L5 region, and standing up.
The patient's partner reported that the patient's pain is exacerbated by standing up.
Test Results: X-ray results were normal.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a probable diagnosis of a low back strain.
Problem list: Low back strain; acute back pain; tingling sensation in the right leg; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg PO BID for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretches and exercises to improve the patient's back condition.
I instructed the patient to rest their back and follow up with me in 2 weeks if the pain does not improve.
I will consider ordering an MRI if the patient's symptoms do not improve after 2 weeks.
Follow-up: The patient is to follow up with me in 2 weeks to re-evaluate their symptoms and consider further testing if necessary.
